scope.notApplicableTo: All other Chapter 1 topics remain outside this baseline. This is a separate, standalone record — it does not extend, merge with, or modify ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json (scope: ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10) or ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, both of which remain entirely separate, unmodified governance records with their own independent version sequences.

filePreservationNote: The approved draft files are unmoved, unrenamed, uncopied, and not promoted into docs/content-design/chapter-01/pilot/. Their internal content and governance fields (blocking, visibility, arabic, etc.) were not altered by this approval. This baseline-approval record, external to the files themselves, is what establishes that the exact versions at the checksums above are approved — mirroring the same pattern already used by ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, which likewise approves the pilot content files in place without relocating or renaming them. The files may continue to be structurally labeled 'draft' internally (generationStatus: "draft-batch1-english-only-generation") even though this external record now marks them baseline-approved.
statementOfApproval: The English-language draft content for ch01-t01 (Fundamental Quantities) and ch01-t04 (Mass, Inertia and Weight), as it exists in the two files listed in approvedDraftFiles at the checksums recorded there, is approved by the project owner as Batch 1 English baseline version 1.0.0. This approval is a project-owner content-readiness decision made on the basis of Claude's completed generation, review, revision, and closure-verification work (see reviewBasis) — it is not an independently derived, third-party human scientific or editorial re-verification beyond that work, and it does not itself authorize Arabic translation, visual production, application integration, or any student-facing use.
approvedCorrectionsNote: Both ch01-corr-001 and ch01-corr-002 carry approvalStatus: "editoriallyApproved" in SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json (ch01-corr-002 with a recorded qualification on 'apparent weight' terminology), with approvals.scientificReviewer left null on both by explicit project-owner instruction. This baseline approval relies on, and does not re-open or re-litigate, those already-approved correction records.

nonBlockingConditions: Consider refining the distance wording in ch01-t01-block-equations to emphasize distance traveled where pedagogically useful.
Consider adding a simple numeric illustration of v = d/t in a future revision, if the audited evidence and topic scope come to support one.
Consider refining ch01-t04-block-explanation's 'ordinary situation' phrasing to specify equilibrium or zero net force where context requires more precision.
Add the ch01-term-mass glossary linkage to ch01-t04-block-equations' glossaryTermIds after glossary approval and identifier registration.

nonBlockingConditionsStatement: The conditions listed under perTopicDecision above are optional future improvements only. They do not block this baseline approval and are not represented as unresolved scientific defects — none was found in the final drafting, review, revision, or closure-verification passes (see reviewBasis). They were not applied as edits during this approval task and may be considered in a future, separate, controlled baseline revision, following the exact revisionLog pattern already established by ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json.

governanceRestrictions.approvalLevelStatement: This is a project-owner ENGLISH-CONTENT baseline approval only. It is distinct from: (1) editorial approval of a correction record (a narrower action already recorded separately in SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json for ch01-corr-001/ch01-corr-002), (2) independent human scientific review (has not occurred, for any topic, at any point in this project), and (3) student-publication authorization (a separate, still-absent gate).
governanceRestrictions.arabicNotAuthorized: Arabic content is not generated or approved by this record. Both approved draft files' Arabic fields remain arabic.translationStatus: "missing" with null text throughout, unchanged by this approval.
governanceRestrictions.visualProductionNotAuthorized: Visual production is not authorized by this record. ch01-t01-visual-001 and ch01-t04-visual-001 remain unproduced (visualGovernance[0].availabilityStatus: "missing") and provisional.
governanceRestrictions.applicationIntegrationNotAuthorized: Application integration is not authorized by this record. PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json's applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds remains exactly the original four pilot topics, unchanged by this approval.

revisionControlPolicy.rule: Any future edit to the English content of ch01-t01 or ch01-t04 (instructorScript, contentBlock, or problem records in the two Batch 1 draft files) must be recorded as a controlled revision against this baseline, not made silently.
revisionControlPolicy.mechanism: A controlled revision increments baselineVersion (e.g. 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0) and appends an entry to revisionLog below recording what changed, why, who authorized it, and when — following the exact pattern already proven twice in ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json's own revisionControlPolicy.revisionLog.

revisionLog.scope: Adds exactly one new contentBlock record, ch01-t01-block-opening (blockType "openingConcept"), as the first record in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. No existing record (ch01-is-101, ch01-t01-block-mainidea, ch01-t01-block-explanation, ch01-t01-block-equations, ch01-t01-block-visual, ch01-t01-block-misconception, ch01-t01-block-review) was changed, reordered relative to each other, or removed. No record in ch01-t04 was touched.
revisionLog.whatChanged: A new opening/introductory content block was added ahead of ch01-t01-block-mainidea, built around a source slide ('1.1 Fundamental Physical Quantities') the project owner supplied directly in the implementation task, together with newly authored explanatory prose (three-basic-aspects framing, direct-vs-combined measurements, distance/time/mass/charge as fundamental foundations, derived-quantity examples including a worked v = d / t = 20 m/s car example, a space-vs-distance misconception, a key-concept summary, and a transition to the units-of-measurement topic). The project-owner-supplied source slide's original English wording is preserved verbatim within the new block's text; the surrounding explanatory paragraphs were authored by Claude directly from the project owner's requested structure, not copied from ch01-t01's other existing blocks.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's seven pre-existing records was reworded, shortened, reordered relative to one another, or removed. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Requested by the project owner as new opening scientific content for the Fundamental Quantities topic, to be integrated into the existing reading flow without duplicating the wording of the already-approved mainIdea/organizedExplanation/misconception blocks.
resolvedGovernanceRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json — new record ch01-t01-block-opening appended at index 0
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic, including the new record; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. Unlike the other revisions logged above, this new record has NOT been through the drafting-authorization / generation / review / revision / closure-verification sequence the rest of this baseline's content went through (see reviewBasis) — its blocking.blockingStatus is 'blocked' with blockingReason including 'scientificReviewPending', and it carries no scientificCorrectionIds, reflecting that its scientific accuracy has been checked only by Claude's own authoring process, not by a separate review pass. This revision authorizes the record's presence in the internal-review application build only, on the same terms as every other blocked/draft record in this file — not any elevated review or approval status beyond that.

revisionLog.scope: Strictly limited to ch01-t01-block-opening's localizedContent.en.text (the explanatory prose added after the verbatim source-slide quotation) in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. The verbatim quoted slide text itself, and every other record in ch01-t01/ch01-t04, is unchanged.
revisionLog.whatChanged: A project-owner scientific/linguistic review of rev-003's explanatory prose (recorded via ch01-english-baseline-rev-003) identified several inaccuracies and revised the wording accordingly: (1) distance/time/mass/charge are now explicitly scoped as 'the quantities this chapter's introductory framework builds its early examples from', not a universal list of every fundamental quantity in physics; (2) the claim that 'every physical quantity...ultimately traces back to' those four was replaced with a context-limited statement; (3) added a new closing 'Scientific note' paragraph stating that in the modern SI system, electric current (not charge) is one of the seven base quantities and charge is derived (Q = I t); (4) 'speed...without needing any unit of its own' was replaced with an explicit statement that speed's derived unit is m/s, formed from distance's and time's own units; (5) 'Saying "far" is not physics; saying "100 m" is' was replaced with a qualitative-vs-quantitative framing; (6) the area/volume/density mentions were trimmed to a brief one-sentence mention (deferred to later sections) rather than developed in this opening slide. The verbatim source-slide quotation at the start of the block is byte-for-byte unchanged from rev-003.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: The verbatim quoted original English slide text ('In physics, there are three basic aspects...distance, time, and two properties of matter called mass and charge.') is unchanged. No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's seven pre-existing records was touched. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Project-owner scientific and linguistic review of the draft-ai-generated-unreviewed content added in rev-003 found it presented a textbook-introductory simplification (charge as a 'fundamental' quantity alongside distance/time/mass) as if it were the complete, universal SI base-quantity list, and contained two overstated/imprecise sentences (the 'no unit of its own' claim for speed, and the 'far is not physics' framing). This revision corrects the wording and adds an explicit SI scientific note, without altering the preserved verbatim slide quotation.
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. This record's blocking.blockingStatus remains 'blocked' (blockingReason still includes 'scientificReviewPending') — this revision is the project owner's own review pass on Claude's authored wording, not the independent third-party scientific review the rest of this baseline's content went through, and does not elevate this record to that status.

revisionLog.scope: Strictly limited to ch01-t01-block-opening's localizedContent.en.text (the explanatory prose after the verbatim source-slide quotation) in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. The verbatim quoted slide text itself, and every other record, is unchanged.
revisionLog.whatChanged: Project-owner pedagogical review of rev-004's wording (now scientifically accurate, per rev-004) found the explanatory prose too long and repetitive for an introductory slide. Restructured into a compact instructional format — Main Idea, five short numbered conceptual steps, Simple Example, Common Misconception, Scientific Note, Connection to the Next Part — replacing the prior sequence of long, loosely-ordered paragraphs. Removed the duplicated explanation of speed's derived unit (m/s, formed from distance's and time's own units), which previously appeared once before the worked example and again after it; the idea is now stated once (in the numbered steps) and the post-example sentence is a short, generic closing ('This example shows how a derived quantity is formed by combining two measured quantities.'). Replaced 'treats distance, time, mass, and charge as the quantities used to describe them in this context' with 'introduces distance, time, mass, and charge as important measurable quantities within its introductory framework'. Replaced 'Distance is the measurable aspect of space.' with 'Distance is one measurable way of describing separation within space.' No scientific idea introduced in rev-004 (the SI base-quantity note, Q = I t, speed's derived unit) was removed — only shortened and de-duplicated.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: The verbatim quoted original English slide text is unchanged. No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's seven pre-existing records was touched. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed. All scientific corrections from rev-004 (SI base-quantity accuracy, Q = I t, speed's derived unit m/s) are preserved, just stated once instead of twice and more concisely.
revisionLog.rationale: Project-owner pedagogical review found the rev-004 wording, while scientifically accurate, read as too dense and repetitive for a short opening slide, risking making the chapter's first concept feel unnecessarily heavy for a beginning student. This revision compacts the structure and removes the one clear duplication (the derived-unit-of-speed explanation) without cutting any scientific content.

governanceStatusChange.field: ch01-t01-block-opening.arabic.translationStatus (Arabic file only; see ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json revisionLog rev-003 for the paired entry)
governanceStatusChange.note: "draft" is the exact, pre-existing status value this file already uses for every one of ch01-t01's other seven records — all of which also went through this project's own generation/review/revision process without becoming 'independently reviewed' or 'approved' in the stricter sense (see governanceRestrictions.independentExpertReviewCompleted: false, unchanged). It was never the case that 'draft' meant 'unreviewed' in this schema; the custom '-ai-generated-unreviewed' suffix was Claude's own invented marker (added when this record was first authored, with no independent or project-owner review yet performed) and is no longer accurate now that the project owner has directly performed two rounds of scientific and linguistic review on this record's wording (rev-004 and this rev-005). This is NOT equivalent to the independent third-party scientific/linguistic review the rest of this baseline's content went through, and does not change blocking.blockingStatus ('blocked', unchanged), studentFacingAllowed (false, unchanged), or studentPublicationAuthorized (false, unchanged).

revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. This record's blocking.blockingStatus remains 'blocked' (blockingReason still includes 'scientificReviewPending') despite the translationStatus wording change described above — that change reflects the project owner's own direct review, not the independent third-party review this baseline's other content went through, and does not itself lift the block.

revisionLog.scope: Strictly limited to ch01-t01-block-opening's localizedContent.en.text (the Scientific Note paragraph only) in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. The verbatim quoted slide text, the Main Idea, all five numbered steps, the Simple Example (including the worked v = d / t = 100 m / 5 s = 20 m/s equation), the Common Misconception, and the Connection to the Next Part are unchanged. No other record in ch01-t01 or ch01-t04 was touched.
revisionLog.whatChanged: Removed the sentence 'Electric charge is a derived quantity, obtained from current multiplied by time: Q = I t.' from the Scientific Note paragraph, and replaced the entire Scientific Note paragraph with a shorter project-owner-authored replacement: 'Scientific Note: This slide follows the textbook's introductory framework for the quantities discussed in this chapter. The complete modern SI system contains seven base quantities.' The prior sentence '...electric current — not electric charge — is one of the seven base quantities, alongside length, time, mass, thermodynamic temperature, amount of substance, and luminous intensity.' was also removed as part of the full-paragraph replacement, since the replacement text states the SI base-quantity count without itemizing the individual quantities or the current-vs-charge contrast.
revisionLog.rationale: Explicit project-owner educational revision: the electric-charge-derivation statement (Q = I t) was deliberately removed because it is outside the instructional scope of this introductory slide, which is meant to introduce space/time/matter and the fundamental-vs-derived-quantity distinction, not the SI current-vs-charge base-quantity distinction.
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false; blocking.blockingStatus on this record remains 'blocked' (blockingReason still includes 'scientificReviewPending'), unchanged by this wording revision.

revisionLog.scope: Adds exactly one new contentBlock record, ch01-t01-block-opening-2 (blockType "openingConceptSlide2"), as the second record in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json (immediately after ch01-t01-block-opening). No existing record (ch01-t01-block-opening, ch01-is-101, ch01-t01-block-mainidea, ch01-t01-block-explanation, ch01-t01-block-equations, ch01-t01-block-visual, ch01-t01-block-misconception, ch01-t01-block-review) was changed, reordered relative to each other, or removed. No record in ch01-t04 was touched.
revisionLog.whatChanged: A second opening/introductory content block was added as a sibling of ch01-t01-block-opening (Slide 1), rendered as 'Slide 2' in the application's Slides section. It presents distance, mass, and time as fundamental quantities via their dimensional symbols (L, M, T), built around a project-owner-supplied source slide ('1.1 Fundamental Physical Quantities: Distance') whose exact original English wording is preserved verbatim within the new block's text, together with the project owner's exact required wording for the Common Misconception, Scientific Note, Key Concept, and Connection to the Next Slide sections. The Main Idea, the five numbered steps (Distance/Mass/Time as L/M/T, derived quantities, and speed as L/T), and the Simple Example (a racing car traveling 120 miles in 2 hours, worked to Speed = 120 miles / 2 h = 60 miles/h) were newly authored by Claude from the task's requested structure, not copied from any other source. A Figure Explanation subsection (an uploaded slide figure showing racing vehicles using parachutes) was requested but omitted from this revision: the referenced image could not be located or extracted in this implementation session, and per the task's own instruction, no image was invented or substituted in its place — the record's blocking.blockingReason includes 'missingVisual' to reflect this until a real image is supplied.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's eight pre-existing records (including ch01-t01-block-opening / Slide 1, entirely byte-for-byte unchanged) was reworded, shortened, reordered relative to one another, or removed. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Requested by the project owner as a second slide for the Fundamental Quantities topic, continuing the Slide 1 pattern to introduce the L/M/T dimensional-symbol framework used throughout the rest of this chapter's mechanics content.
resolvedGovernanceRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json — new record ch01-t01-block-opening-2 appended at index 1
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic, including the new record; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. This new record has NOT been through the drafting-authorization / generation / review / revision / closure-verification sequence the rest of this baseline's content went through — its blocking.blockingStatus is 'blocked' with blockingReason including 'scientificReviewPending' and 'missingVisual', reflecting that its scientific accuracy has been checked only by Claude's own authoring process (not a separate review pass) and that its figure is not yet available. This revision authorizes the record's presence in the internal-review application build only, on the same terms as every other blocked/draft record in this file.

revisionLog.scope: Four corrections to ch01-t01-block-opening-2 (Slide 2) and one structural/architecture change applied to both ch01-t01-block-opening (Slide 1) and ch01-t01-block-opening-2 (Slide 2) in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. No instructional prose in ch01-t01-block-opening (Slide 1) was changed — only its blockType/slide-metadata tagging (see 'Architecture change' below). No record in ch01-t04 was touched.
revisionLog.whatChanged: (1) Scientific correction — length vs. distance: in Slide 2's Step 1, 'L represents the dimension of length or distance.' was replaced with 'L represents the dimension of length. Distance is one physical quantity that has the dimension of length.', clarifying that length is the base dimension and distance is a quantity that has that dimension. The Main Idea sentence was revised from 'combining three fundamental quantities: distance, mass, and time' to 'using the three base dimensions of length, mass, and time.' The Key Concept sentence's 'Distance, mass, and time form the dimensional foundation...' was revised to 'Length, mass, and time form the dimensional foundation...' for the same reason. The original quoted slide text ('Distance (L)', 'For example, speed has the unit of miles per hour (L/T).') was NOT altered. (2) Governance correction — missingVisual removed: 'missingVisual' was removed from ch01-t01-block-opening-2's blocking.blockingReason. The Figure Explanation subsection was intentionally omitted by explicit project-owner decision because the figure is illustrative and not required for understanding Slide 2's concept — not because the image remains an unresolved gap. No provisional visual record was created; provenanceLinks[0].evidence now documents this explicit decision. All other blocking reasons (translationPending, scientificReviewPending, other) and all publication flags are unchanged. (3) Architecture change — reusable slide collection: the blockType of both ch01-t01-block-opening and ch01-t01-block-opening-2 was changed from the per-slide values 'openingConcept'/'openingConceptSlide2' to one generic, reusable 'slide' blockType, and each record gained slideNumber (1 and 2 respectively) and slideTitleEn fields recording its display order and bilingual title. This is a schema/tagging change only — no character of either slide's approved instructional text was altered by it, and both blockIds (ch01-t01-block-opening, ch01-t01-block-opening-2) are unchanged. The application's normalized model now exposes topic.slides as an ordered array (see src/types/normalized.ts's NormalizedSlide / src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides), so a future Slide 3 requires only a new 'slide'-typed record with the next slideNumber — no new ContentBlockType, no new NormalizedTopic field, no adapter or TopicPage wiring specific to that slide.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: No word of Slide 1's approved instructional prose was reworded, shortened, reordered, or removed — only its blockType tag and new slideNumber/slideTitleEn metadata fields were added, alongside its existing, unchanged blockId and text. Slide 2's original verbatim quotation, all five step titles, the Simple Example calculation, the Common Misconception's core claim, the Scientific Note's mechanics-only scope, and the Connection sentence are otherwise unchanged. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Project-owner scientific, governance, and architecture review of the initial Slide 2 addition (rev-007) identified: a conflation of the length dimension with the distance quantity in the newly authored explanation; an unnecessary 'missingVisual' blocking reason for a figure the project owner decided is not instructionally required; and a schema design (one new ContentBlockType and one new NormalizedTopic field per slide) that would not scale to a third slide without further schema changes. This revision applies the project owner's exact corrected wording, removes the unneeded blocking reason with its rationale documented in provenance, and replaces the per-slide schema with a generic, reusable slide collection.
resolvedGovernanceRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json — ch01-t01-block-opening.blockType/slideNumber/slideTitleEn added; ch01-t01-block-opening-2.localizedContent.en.text (Step 1, Main Idea, Key Concept) and blocking.blockingReason revised, blockType/slideNumber/slideTitleEn added
docs/content-design/chapter-01/IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json — both ch01-t01-block-opening and ch01-t01-block-opening-2 entries updated (blockType, checksums)
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. ch01-t01-block-opening-2's blocking.blockingStatus remains 'blocked' (blockingReason now translationPending, scientificReviewPending, other — missingVisual removed per the explicit project-owner decision described above, not because review status changed). ch01-t01-block-opening's blocking fields are entirely unchanged.

revisionLog.scope: Adds exactly one new contentBlock record, ch01-t01-block-opening-3 (blockType "slide", slideNumber 3), as the third record in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json (immediately after ch01-t01-block-opening-2). No existing record (ch01-t01-block-opening, ch01-t01-block-opening-2, ch01-is-101, ch01-t01-block-mainidea, ch01-t01-block-explanation, ch01-t01-block-equations, ch01-t01-block-visual, ch01-t01-block-misconception, ch01-t01-block-review) was changed, reordered relative to each other, or removed. No record in ch01-t04 was touched.
revisionLog.whatChanged: A third opening/introductory content block was added as a sibling of ch01-t01-block-opening (Slide 1) and ch01-t01-block-opening-2 (Slide 2), rendered as 'Slide 3' via the existing generic topic.slides[] collection — no new ContentBlockType, no new NormalizedTopic field, no Slide-3-specific adapter branch or TopicPage conditional was added (see the reusable-slide architecture established by rev-008). It presents distance measurement in metric and English units, built around a project-owner-uploaded source slide image (a table titled 'Physical Quantity / Metric Units / English Units') whose exact original English prose ('1.1 Fundamental Physical Quantities: Distance...') is preserved verbatim within the new block's text, and whose table data is reconstructed as a real semantic <table> (headers/rows) rather than the screenshot itself, added via new generic tableEn/tableAr fields on ContentBlockRecord (see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's SourceTable) — a mechanism available to any content-block record, not specific to this slide. The Main Idea, the five numbered steps (one-dimensional measurement; length/width/height as distance; symbol conventions d/l/w/h/L; metric powers-of-ten; English-unit relationships), the Simple Example (a 2 m x 80 cm x 75 cm table, with the unit-consistency conversion 2 m = 200 cm and a distinct '200 cm x 80 cm x 75 cm' calculation block), the new Table Explanation subsection (clarifying that same-row units are not equivalent conversions, e.g. 1 m != 1 ft and 1 cm != 1 mi), the Common Misconception, the Scientific Note, the Key Concept, and the Connection to the Next Slide were authored/translated by Claude from the project owner's requested structure and exact required wording, not copied from any other source.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's nine pre-existing records (including Slides 1 and 2, entirely byte-for-byte unchanged) was reworded, shortened, reordered relative to one another, or removed. The uploaded source table's rows and empty cells are preserved exactly as reconstructed, not reordered or reinterpreted. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Requested by the project owner as a third slide for the Fundamental Quantities topic, extending the reusable slide collection established by rev-008 to demonstrate that a new slide requires only a new source record — proving out the generic architecture in practice.
resolvedGovernanceRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json — new record ch01-t01-block-opening-3 appended at index 2
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic, including the new record; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. This new record has NOT been through the drafting-authorization / generation / review / revision / closure-verification sequence the rest of this baseline's content went through — its blocking.blockingStatus is 'blocked' with blockingReason including 'scientificReviewPending', reflecting that its scientific accuracy and translation have been checked only by Claude's own authoring process, not a separate review pass. Unlike Slide 2's original addition, no 'missingVisual' reason is present: the uploaded source table was fully available and used (reconstructed as a semantic table), so no visual asset remains missing. This revision authorizes the record's presence in the internal-review application build only, on the same terms as every other blocked/draft record in this file.

revisionLog.scope: Three pedagogical wording corrections to ch01-t01-block-opening-3's (Slide 3) localizedContent.en.text (Simple Example, the dimensions calculation block, and the Scientific Note) in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. The original quoted slide text, the reconstructed source table, the five numbered steps, the Table Explanation, the Common Misconception, the Key Concept, and the Connection section were NOT touched. No record in ch01-t01 other than Slide 3, and no record in ch01-t04, was touched.
revisionLog.whatChanged: (1) The Simple Example's unit-consistency sentence was revised from 'Before comparing or combining them, their units must be made consistent: 2 m = 200 cm.' to 'Before comparing these measurements or using them together in a calculation, their units should be made consistent: 2 m = 200 cm.' (2) The bare calculation paragraph '200 cm × 80 cm × 75 cm' was relabeled 'Dimensions: 200 cm × 80 cm × 75 cm' to avoid implying an unlabeled multiplication result, and a new clarifying sentence was added immediately after it: 'The multiplication signs separate the three dimensions here; no volume calculation is required in this example.' (3) The Scientific Note was refined from 'Millimeter, centimeter, and kilometer are metric multiples or submultiples of the meter.' to the more precise 'The millimeter and centimeter are decimal submultiples of the meter, while the kilometer is a decimal multiple of it.'
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: The verbatim quoted original English slide text ('1.1 Fundamental Physical Quantities: Distance...'), the reconstructed source table (tableEn, unchanged), all five numbered steps, the Table Explanation subsection, the Common Misconception, the Key Concept, and the Connection to the Next Slide are unchanged. Slides 1 and 2 (ch01-t01-block-opening, ch01-t01-block-opening-2) are entirely untouched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Project-owner pedagogical wording review of Slide 3 (rev-009) found: the original unit-consistency sentence read ambiguously ('comparing or combining' could suggest arithmetic combination beyond unit conversion); the bare '200 cm × 80 cm × 75 cm' calculation block, with no label, could be misread by a student as an implied volume calculation, which is outside this example's scope (the example only demonstrates unit consistency across three distance measurements, not a volume computation); and the Scientific Note's 'metric multiples or submultiples' phrasing did not specify which unit was a multiple versus a submultiple. This revision clarifies all three without altering any scientific claim already present.
resolvedGovernanceRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json — ch01-t01-block-opening-3.localizedContent.en.text revised (Simple Example, dimensions block, Scientific Note)
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. ch01-t01-block-opening-3's blocking.blockingStatus remains 'blocked' (blockingReason still translationPending, scientificReviewPending, other — no missingVisual, unchanged from rev-009), unaffected by this wording-only revision. Slides 1 and 2's governance fields are entirely unchanged.

identifierRegistrationStatus.statement: Identifier registration in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json is NOT a prerequisite for this internal English baseline approval and was NOT performed as part of it. Registration is required before any future application-integration step. IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json was not modified by this approval.

downstreamStatus.applicationIntegrationStatus: notAuthorized; also not mechanically possible today, since docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ is outside apps/chapter1-mvp/'s current vite.config.ts server.fs.allow / rawImports.ts import scope

unchangedByThisApproval: Every instructorScript, contentBlock, and problem record in the two Batch 1 draft files remains in its existing draft/blocked governance state — blocking.blockingStatus, blocking.studentFacingAllowed, arabic.translationStatus, and every other governance/localization field on each record are untouched by this approval.
The two draft files themselves are unmoved, unrenamed, uncopied, unmodified, and remain at docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/.
ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json and ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json remain entirely separate and unmodified by this record.
PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json's scope.authorizedTopicIds and applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds remain exactly the original four pilot topics.
studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic.
canonicalGenerationAuthorized remains chapter-wide false in PILOT_READINESS.json.
This approval does not authorize Arabic translation, visual production, or application integration for ch01-t01 or ch01-t04.
